summary: Shapiro expresses frustration with a woodworking magazine's unhelpful router bit review that offered no specific data or rankings, only the vague conclusion that expensive bits cut better. He then shares a useful review from Fine Woodworker that includes actual comparative data. The post highlights that mid-priced Oldham Viper bits from Home Depot performed well, while some expensive brands ranked near the bottom. It's a brief, practical maker-community post from early in Shapiro's blogging history.
key_points: A major woodworking magazine published a router bit review with no rankings, no brand list, and no useful data — just the conclusion that expensive bits are better
Fine Woodworker published a data-driven review with an actual scorecard comparing brands
Budget-friendly Oldham Viper bits from Home Depot turned out to be a strong performer, while some expensive brands ranked poorly
money_quotes: More expensive bits cut better. Saywhat?
Just a looong section on methadology, and the conclusion that you should Fedex your visa card overnight to the good people at Carbide Anonymous and be thankful for it. Bull.
So when I stumbled on a non-moronic review of router bits yesterday I thought I should share.
It turns out that the yellow Oldham Viper bits they sell at the Depot are a solid deal (and, bonus, made in the USA).
key_insight: Good product reviews require actual data and rankings, not just the lazy conclusion that you get what you pay for.
